mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market conditions that support corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of PAYEMS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates monetary easing, reducing discount rates for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or FEDFUNDS declines without corresponding SPX gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weak aggregate demand, creating downside pressure on corporate profits and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and HOUST both fall > 1.5% YoY
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where GDPC1 and HOUST both decline, or the interaction effect's t-statistic < 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3% YoY) forces central banks to maintain restrictive monetary policy, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances with CPIAUCSL > 3% YoY or inflation fails to predict SPX direction (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 60%+ of INDPRO growth periods or INDPRO fails to predict SPX direction (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A surge in consumer spending (RSAFS) relative to GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong demand dynamics, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS/GDPC1 ratio rises or the interaction effect's t-statistic < 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 3.5% signals labor market tightness, increasing wage pressures and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of UNRATE < 3.5% periods or unemployment fails to predict SPX direction (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate manufacturing contraction, reducing corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of INDPRO decline periods or INDPRO fails to predict SPX direction (Rank IC < 0.03).
